article_meta.title: War in UkraineKremlin says leaked U.S

reasoning: Notable attribution violation, multiple mislabeled/weak items, and missed clearer author-prose bias. Also used markdown fences instead of a raw JSON array.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Flagged a Kremlin-attributed claim as if it lacked attribution, violating the rule against treating sourced statements as author bias.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Two of three items are weak or incorrect: one is clearly attributed (not author bias), and another mismatches the cited text and rationale.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed defensible instances in author prose such as 'maximalist demands' and the evaluative 'appeared to favor Moscow heavily' (which was not correctly analyzed).
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels largely off: 'Omission of Source Attribution' where attribution exists, 'Bias by Omission' used where the issue (if any) would be subjective wording; only the 'Unsubstantiated Claims' item is reasonably labeled.
